title: onobrychis alba var. striatula
intro: Onobrychis alba var. striatula, also known as white sainfoin, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and polyphenolic compounds, which have been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood sugar levels, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress. White sainfoin can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and fever.
